comments.author: @TruePlanetOffical
comments.text:
Did anything in this video surprise you the most?
👇 Share your thoughts below — I'd love to hear what stood out to you!

comments.author: @shirleymillar10
comments.text: The view of two bull moose was in front of the Grand Tetons in Wyoming....

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: How were you able to spot such a small detail? According to the footage source we found, that location was listed as Arkansas. Do those two places look similar, or was the footage labeled incorrectly?

comments.author: @karamalazee9176
comments.text: Spectacular.. much information .. great video.. keep going

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you so much! I really appreciate you watching and taking the time to leave such kind words. What part of the video stood out to you the most?

comments.author: @TRUEGLOBAL-p7b
comments.text: Respect to the people who adapt to such challenging environments.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you for saying that. The resilience of people who live in these environments is truly remarkable. Adapting to difficult conditions while continuing to build communities, raise families, and preserve local traditions deserves a lot of respect. Is there a particular place or community in the video that impressed you the most?

comments.author: @Mo-zn9zz
comments.text: European colonial settlers genocided the indigenous people to Arkansas and stoke their lives and lands.... dont rewrite history to ommit these atrocities

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective. History is complex, and it's important to acknowledge both the beauty of a place and the difficult chapters that helped shape it. Is there a particular event or story from Arkansas's history that stands out to you?

comments.author: @sirene0112
comments.text: Why do you see Diamond Head in Hawaii instead crater of diamond?

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Great question! The name "Diamond Head" actually comes from British sailors who mistook sparkling calcite crystals in the rocks for diamonds. It's really a volcanic crater, but the historic name stuck. Have you ever visited Hawaii yourself?

comments.author: @sonjie33
comments.text: Where are you getting your information from? There are no moose in Arkansas. They are only elk and you are not showing an elk. You are showing a moose.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: @RodgerW6 You're right, That's definitely a moose, and I should have caught that during editing. My apologies, and thanks for speaking up.

replies.author: @TruePlanetOffical
replies.text: Good catch! Thanks for pointing that out. I’ll go back and double-check that section. If that’s a moose, then it was definitely an editing mistake on my end. What part of Arkansas are you from?
